title: Dependency Trap
description: A pathological dependency benchmark where a publish-like task looks tempting but a cyclic gate makes at least one dependency violation unavoidable.
goal: Show dependency-violation accounting instead of pretending every graph is clean.
expected_constraints: Show that high priority does not erase dependency penalties.
Persist approvals for the risky cycle tasks.
notes: The cycle is deliberate. The benchmark is designed to test reporting, not to model a valid release plan.

tasks.title: Ship hotfix
tasks.description: Prepare the high-value hotfix publish action.

tasks.title: Approve hotfix
tasks.description: Gate shipping behind an approval record.

tasks.title: Inspect release state
tasks.description: Inspect the repository before reasoning about the hotfix.

tasks.title: Document cycle risk
tasks.description: Explain why the cyclic gate makes the graph invalid.

context_candidates.content: A task with unmet dependencies should carry an explicit penalty.
High-risk publish actions must be approval gated.
A noisy discussion that does not help resolve the dependency cycle.
